title: All-role YouTube access, banned/suspended error screens, and YouTube OAuth fix

requirements.text: Remove any role-based access restrictions from YouTube-related features (channel connection, video URL input, YouTube player, clip suggestions, clip creation, post-to-YouTube). All authenticated users regardless of role (owner, admin, user, friend) must be able to see and use these features.

quotes: Make it so all roles can access the YouTube stuff

acceptanceCriteria: A user with role 'user' can see and interact with the ChannelConnection, VideoUrlForm, YouTubePlayer, ClipSuggestions, ClipTimestampControls, and ClipList components
A user with role 'friend' can see and interact with the same YouTube-related components
No YouTube-related UI element is hidden or disabled based on role checks alone

requirements.text: When an authenticated user's account status is 'banned' or 'suspended', display a full-screen error/blocking page instead of the normal app UI. The screen must clearly communicate that the account is banned or suspended and prevent access to all app features.

quotes: make it so that banned and suspended shows a error screen to the people with the status

acceptanceCriteria: A user whose backend status is 'banned' sees a dedicated error screen explaining their account is banned
A user whose backend status is 'suspended' sees a dedicated error screen explaining their account is suspended
The error screen prevents access to all other app functionality (no navigation around it)
The error screen is shown immediately after login if the status is banned or suspended
Users with active (non-banned, non-suspended) status see the normal app UI

requirements.text: Fix the YouTube OAuth connection flow so that after a user completes the Google OAuth callback, the channel remains connected and does not revert to a disconnected state. Ensure the OAuth credentials are correctly stored in the backend, the actor is fully initialized before storing credentials, and all relevant React Query caches (channel connection status, OAuth credential status) are invalidated after a successful credential save so the UI reflects the connected state.

quotes: fix the YouTube connection because it still isn't working

acceptanceCriteria: After completing the Google OAuth flow, the ChannelConnection component shows the connected channel info and does not revert to disconnected
The OAuthCallback page waits for the backend actor to be fully ready before attempting to store credentials
On successful credential storage, the useYouTubeChannel and OAuth credential status queries are invalidated and refetched
The backend correctly persists OAuth credentials across calls so isYouTubeConnected returns true after credentials are saved
No race condition exists between actor initialization and credential storage in the OAuth callback

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
The banned/suspended error screen must be enforced at the app layout level so it cannot be bypassed by direct navigation
nonGoals: Adding new YouTube features beyond what already exists
Changing the admin panel's ability to set user status
Modifying the role permission model beyond removing YouTube access restrictions
